name: Multi-Factor Authentication Request Generation
description: Adversaries send repeated MFA push notifications or phone calls to a target user hoping the user approves one out of fatigue (MFA fatigue/prompt bombing). In Entra ID, each push attempt generates a SigninLogs entry with ResultType 50074 (strong authentication required) or ResultType 0 if the user approves. The adversary typically already has valid credentials (obtained via password spray or phishing) and is blocked only by MFA. Microsoft Authenticator's number matching feature and additional context mitigate this but are not universally enabled.
iocs: SigninLogs high volume of ResultType 50074 ('User did not pass the MFA challenge') for a single UserPrincipalName within a 30-minute window
SigninLogs alternating ResultType 50074 and 50076 ('User did not pass MFA') for the same account over an extended period — persistent fatigue campaign
SigninLogs showing ResultType 0 following a series of ResultType 50074 events from the same IPAddress — successful MFA fatigue approval
SigninLogs push MFA requests originating from unusual geographic location or time-of-day for the target user
SigninLogs MFA requests targeting privileged accounts (detectable by correlating UserPrincipalName with AuditLogs role membership)
SigninLogs AuthenticationDetails showing 'phoneAppNotification' as the MFA method with repeated failure status — push bombing pattern
Multiple distinct UserPrincipalName values each receiving MFA push attempts from the same IPAddress — targeted multi-account fatigue campaign
evasion_variants: Space MFA push attempts 60 seconds apart to stay under per-minute rate-limit detection thresholds
Combine MFA fatigue with social engineering — call the target claiming to be IT support requiring them to approve an MFA notification
Target the user late at night or early morning when fatigue or confusion is highest and they may approve without scrutiny
Send exactly 3 MFA requests per hour to avoid patterns that trigger 'excessive MFA failures' alert rules with higher thresholds
Alternate between push notification and phone call MFA methods to maximize disruption and coverage if the user dismisses one type
Target accounts where MFA is configured for phone call rather than authenticator app — phone calls are harder to quickly dismiss
detection_kql_hints: SigninLogs | where ResultType in (50074, 50076) | summarize MFAFailures=count() by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m) | where MFAFailures > 5

SigninLogs | where ResultType in (50074, 50076) | order by TimeGenerated asc | extend Success = next(ResultType) == 0 and next(UserPrincipalName) == UserPrincipalName | where Success == true | project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress
SigninLogs | where AuthenticationDetails has 'phoneAppNotification' and ResultType != 0 | summarize PushCount=count() by UserPrincipalName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where PushCount > 10
SigninLogs | where ResultType == 50074 | summarize dcount(UserPrincipalName) by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h) | where dcount_UserPrincipalName > 3

SigninLogs | where ResultType == 50074 | extend HourOfDay = hourofday(TimeGenerated) | where HourOfDay < 6 or HourOfDay > 22 | summarize count() by UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
